questions.question: What is the formal definition of f(n) = O(g(n))?
questions.answer: ∃ constants c > 0 and n₀ ≥ 1 such that for all n ≥ n₀, f(n) ≤ c·g(n)

questions.question: Apply the Master Theorem to T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n). What is the result?
What is amortised analysis and how does it differ from worst-case analysis?
questions.answer: Amortised measures average cost per operation over a sequence; worst-case measures a single operation's worst possible cost. Amortised is a guarantee over n operations, not a probability.

questions.question: If n ≤ 10⁵, which is the slowest algorithm that will still pass in 1 second?
questions.answer: O(n log n) — approximately 1.7×10⁶ operations, well within 10⁸
